_comment: Payload variant for AML.T0086 / mcp_server, exercising the SSRF shape. Used by runbooks/dvaa/L3-02__ssrf-via-mcp.md against DVAA's ToolBot, which exposes a fetch_url MCP tool that does not validate the URL target. The path-traversal variant lives in dvaa_l2_03_path_traversal.json — same atomic, different exfil shape.
rationale: When an MCP-exposed HTTP-fetch tool accepts arbitrary URLs without validation, the attacker can coerce the agent (or directly invoke the tool) to fetch from internal endpoints unreachable from the public internet — cloud metadata services, internal admin panels, RFC1918 hosts. The path-traversal variant reads filesystem; the SSRF variant reads network. Both are T0086 Exfiltration via AI Agent Tool, different exfil channels.
alternative_targets: file:///etc/passwd (some HTTP libs accept file:// — combines path traversal + SSRF)
